stages.name: Discover
activities: Reads README.md to understand the 6 primitives and two-layer architecture
Views the code examples showing query/waitForPageReady/actAndWait patterns
Understands the '7 testing pain points' table and maps to own testing frustrations
Sees the '60 pages in 15-30 min vs 2+ hours' efficiency claim
friction_points: README examples use page.probe shorthand but the actual Playwright integration requires ProbePage wrapper — the setup step is not shown
No quick-start tutorial scoped to a specific framework (React+Playwright, Flutter, etc.)
README platform coverage table shows 'Phase X' status labels that don't clearly map to 'can I use this today?'

stages.name: Evaluate
activities: Reads DESIGN.md for full API surface and linkage model details
Reviews example project for own platform to see annotation patterns
Checks spec/probe-types.ts to understand the type contract
Compares against current test approach to estimate friction reduction
friction_points: DESIGN.md is written in Chinese — English developers need translation assistance
Example projects are not runnable yet (depend on SDK builds not published)
No benchmark data or case study comparing probe vs pure Playwright selector approach

stages.name: Integrate
activities: Installs integration package (@allforai/ui-test-probe-playwright)
Builds sdk/web IIFE bundle (npm run build in sdk/web/) as prerequisite
Adds injectProbe() to test setup / playwright.config.ts global fixtures
Wraps Playwright Page in ProbePage for typed probe access
Registers custom matchers (toHaveProbeState, toHaveProbeData, toBeEffectivelyVisible) via expect.extend()
friction_points: Must build sdk/web IIFE bundle first — dist/probe-bundle.js dependency is not pre-published
No documented global setup pattern for Playwright fixtures
ProbePage is not automatically injected — requires explicit new ProbePage(page) in each test

stages.name: Use
activities: Writes tests using probe.query() for state assertions instead of screenshots
Replaces page.waitForTimeout() with probe.waitForPageReady()
Uses probe.actAndWait() for interaction + synchronization in one call
Uses probe.verifyLinkage() to validate data flow chains
Uses probe.runAcrossDevices() for responsive testing across device presets
Uses snapshot()/diff() to create regression baselines
friction_points: Requires frontend developer to have added annotations before query() returns data
If annotation missing, query() returns null with no guidance on what annotation to add
actAndWait() action string parsing ('select:completed') is convention-based — easy to misformat
verifyLinkage() timeout is fixed at 5000ms in web SDK — no option to configure

stages.name: Extend
activities: Creates domain-specific test utilities composing probe methods
Builds custom Playwright matchers beyond the 3 built-in ones
Uses snapshot/diff for regression testing baselines
friction_points: No documented plugin or extension API
Snapshot format may change between versions with no migration guide

stages.name: Contribute
activities: Reports issues with platform-specific behavior
Proposes new ProbeType or LinkageEffect additions via GitHub
friction_points: No CONTRIBUTING.md with contribution guidelines
No conformance test suite to validate cross-platform consistency (referenced in DESIGN.md but not present)
Spec change process is informal

stages.name: Discover
activities: Learns about probe annotation from test engineer or README
Understands the annotation cost table from DESIGN.md section 5 (5-30 min per page)
Confirms that collector is only injected at test time — zero production impact
friction_points: DESIGN.md is in Chinese — no standalone English annotation guide exists
README does not clearly explain that only the annotation layer is added to production code

stages.name: Evaluate
activities: Reviews platform-specific annotation table in DESIGN.md section 4
Reviews example OrderPage for own platform
Understands linkage declaration format from DESIGN.md examples
friction_points: 9 ProbeTypes available but no decision guide for which to pick for a given widget
Linkage JSON format is complex — no schema validation or IDE autocomplete available
No examples of linkage declarations for computed or store path types

stages.name: Integrate
activities: Runs CLI instrument command to auto-annotate an existing codebase
Manually adds/refines probe annotations to key controls (5-10 per page)
Declares linkage targets for elements that drive downstream elements
Runs CLI validate to check for annotation errors
friction_points: CLI instrument auto-generates IDs that may be generic (counter strategy) rather than semantic
Linkage path type selection (direct vs api vs store) requires understanding the data flow architecture
No IDE plugin for attribute autocomplete or inline validation

stages.name: Use
activities: Runs CLI validate to check for duplicate IDs, orphan linkages, circular linkages
Reviews validate output and fixes annotation issues
Runs CLI report to see per-page and per-type annotation coverage
Keeps annotations updated as components evolve
friction_points: Validate is static analysis only — does not catch runtime-generated component annotation issues
No warning when refactoring renames IDs that linkage targets reference
Keeping linkage declarations in sync with actual data flow requires manual discipline

stages.name: Extend
activities: Adds annotations for new features/components
Maintains linkage declarations as data flow architecture changes
Uses dev-forge for auto-generated code that includes annotations from the start
friction_points: No refactoring-aware tooling to update linkage targets when element IDs change

stages.name: Contribute
activities: Proposes new annotation attributes for domain-specific needs
Reports issues with CLI instrument patterns for specific frameworks
friction_points: No formal process for proposing spec attribute additions

stages.name: Discover
activities: Reads README.md and DESIGN.md section 4 (cross-platform SDK architecture diagram)
Reviews .allforai/product-map/source-summary.json for module structure
Identifies the 3-layer pattern: annotations → collector → actions
friction_points: No SDK porting guide or CONTRIBUTING.md
DESIGN.md is in Chinese
ProbeType enum divergence (7 vs 9 types in existing platforms) is undocumented

stages.name: Evaluate
activities: Reads spec/probe-types.ts as the interface contract
Reads sdk/web/ as the reference implementation in detail
Reads an existing platform SDK (e.g. sdk/flutter/) to understand adaptation patterns
Identifies which UITestProbe methods are most complex to implement on target platform
friction_points: No conformance test suite to know when implementation is complete
getOverlaps() has no clear equivalent on native platforms
snapshot/diff are only implemented on web — unclear if these are required

stages.name: Integrate
activities: Scaffolds sdk/{platform}/ directory structure (annotations/, collector/, actions/)
Ports ProbeElement model to target language
Implements AnnotationParser equivalent reading platform-native metadata
Implements ElementRegistry using platform tree scanning APIs
Implements StateObserver using platform change notification APIs
Implements ActionDispatcher with 5-point pre-check pattern
friction_points: Pre-check pattern must be faithfully reproduced — NOT_FOUND/NOT_VISIBLE/DISABLED/BUSY/OPTION_NOT_FOUND error codes must match
MutationObserver (web) has no direct equivalent on native platforms — must use polling or platform notification APIs
Source tracking (network interception) is complex and varies significantly per platform

stages.name: Use
activities: Runs example project tests against own implementation
Tests each ProbeType annotation and verifies ProbeElement fields are populated correctly
Tests pre-check error paths (disabled element, loading element, missing element)
friction_points: Example projects are not runnable yet
No cross-platform comparison test to verify field-level parity

stages.name: Extend
activities: Adds platform-specific optimizations (e.g. native accessibility APIs for better metadata)
Documents any known divergences from spec
friction_points: No versioning strategy for spec changes that may require SDK updates

stages.name: Contribute
activities: Submits PR with new platform SDK
Updates source-summary.json and file-catalog.json
Adds example project for the new platform
friction_points: No automated CI to run cross-platform conformance checks on PR
